name: Howard's Persian Sweet Rice
recipeIngredient: Water as needed for the juice of 1 orange
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the zest from an orange using a swivel-bladed peeler, aiming for the thinnest part of the skin with as little white pith as possible. Trim any ragged edges and slice into long, thin strips. Set aside.
2. Squeeze the juice from the orange and strain it. Add enough water to reach a total volume of 1.5 cups. Set aside.
3. Soak raisins in water until covered, or soak them in about 1 tablespoon of sherry.
4. Shred the carrot using a food processor or hand grater. Set aside.
5. Melt 2 tablespoons of butter or oil in the pot used for cooking the rice. Place the pot on low heat. When the oil begins to bubble, add the almonds and stir-fry briefly until they turn golden brown. Remove the almonds and set them aside.
6. Add the remaining liquid mixture (from step 2) and carrots to the pot. Stir-fry the carrots for 2-3 minutes; they will absorb most of the oil in the pot.
7. Add sugar, salt, orange zest, and the reserved raisins to the pot. Increase the heat and bring the mixture to a boil.
8. Once boiling, add the rice. Remove the carrots from the pot and add them back in.
